title: Royal Australian Air Force plane in rare visit to Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: A special aircraft from down under was a rare visitor to Prestwick Airport earlier this week.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The Royal Australian Air Force Airbus A330-200MRTT turned heads as it made a brief refuelling stop (Image: Martin Clarke) This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The Royal Australian Air Force Airbus A330-200MRTT turned heads as it made a brief refuelling stop at the airport on Thursday, March 5. The KC-30A Multi-Role Tanker Transport (MRTT) is a heavily modified Airbus A330 airliner that allows the Royal Australian Air Force to conduct air-to-air refuelling and provide strategic air lift.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Special aircraft from down under is rare visitor to Prestwick Airport
